The boxer is known for his incredible power.
He fights with an aggressive style.
His opponents fear his heavy blows.
The first match was a huge event.
He landed exactly one hundred twenty punches.
That number was recorded by the officials.
The crowd cheered for every single hit.
The second match brought new excitement.
He improved his performance significantly.
His punch count increased by twenty five percent.
We must calculate that new total now.
Twenty five percent of one hundred twenty is thirty.
Adding thirty to one hundred twenty gives one hundred fifty.
So he landed one hundred fifty punches in match two.
The third match was the final showdown.
He aimed to break his previous record.
He landed thirty more punches than before.
One hundred fifty plus thirty equals one hundred eighty.
That was the total for the third fight.
Now we need the grand total for all matches.
The first match contributed one hundred twenty punches.
The second match added one hundred fifty punches.
The third match provided one hundred eighty punches.
We must add these three numbers together.
One hundred twenty plus one hundred fifty is two hundred seventy.
Two hundred seventy plus one hundred eighty is four hundred fifty.
The total count is four hundred fifty power punches.
This is a remarkable achievement for any fighter.
His power punches defined the entire series.
The numbers show a clear upward trend.
Each match saw an increase in his output.
Fans will remember these stats for years.
The boxer proved his dominance in the ring.
Four hundred fifty is the final answer here.
